title: End-of-year International Education Showcase — Grades 4–6
subtitle: Bilingual student projects fill the school hall for the annual showcase
excerpt: On the last Friday of the school year, our Grade 4 to 6 students opened their classrooms to families for the annual International Education Showcase. Each class presented a bilingual project — from poster boards on global water access to short documentary films about Taoyuan's international transport links. The hall was packed, the English was impressive, and the pride on every student's face said it all.
paragraphs: On the last Friday of the school year, our Grade 4 to 6 students opened their classrooms to families for the annual International Education Showcase. Each class presented a bilingual project — from poster boards on global water access to short documentary films about Taoyuan's international transport links.

Students worked in small groups over the course of the semester, researching topics that connected local life in Taoyuan to wider global issues. One Grade 5 team built a working model of a water filtration system after researching access to clean water in different countries. A Grade 6 team produced a five-minute documentary, filmed partly on location at THSR Taoyuan Station, explaining how the high-speed rail network connects Taiwan's cities.

Parents and grandparents moved between classrooms throughout the afternoon, asking students questions in English about their projects. Several students mentioned afterwards that explaining their work out loud, in English, to an adult who wasn't their teacher was the most challenging — and most rewarding — part of the whole project.

The hall was packed, the English was impressive, and the pride on every student's face said it all. Teachers are already planning next year's showcase theme around sustainability and renewable energy.

title: Three prizes at Taoyuan Bilingual Science Competition
subtitle: Grade 5 and 6 teams compete entirely in English
excerpt: Our Grade 5 and 6 science teams brought home a gold, silver, and special jury prize — presenting their research entirely in English.
paragraphs: Our Grade 5 and 6 science teams brought home a gold, silver, and special jury prize from this year's Taoyuan City Bilingual Science Competition — presenting their research entirely in English to a panel of judges from across the district.

The gold medal went to a Grade 6 team investigating water quality in local irrigation channels near the school, using simple testing kits to measure pH and turbidity over several weeks. The team presented their findings as a full English-language poster, fielding follow-up questions from judges with confidence.

A Grade 5 team earned silver for their project on solar panel efficiency, building a small working model to test how panel angle affects energy output throughout the day. The special jury prize recognised a Grade 6 team's creative approach to studying plant growth under different light wavelengths.

Teachers noted that competing in English added an extra layer of challenge beyond the science itself, but also gave students valuable practice presenting technical ideas clearly to people outside their usual classroom.

title: Grade 3 explores the Airport MRT — in English
subtitle: A field trip that doubled as a real-world English lesson
excerpt: Students navigated Taoyuan Airport MRT from A18 to A1, asking staff questions in English and writing bilingual travel journals on the way back.
paragraphs: Students navigated Taoyuan Airport MRT from A18 to A1, asking staff questions in English and writing bilingual travel journals on the way back. The trip was part of the Grade 3 'All Together' integrated activities unit, which connects classroom English to real community experiences.

Before boarding, students practised key phrases they would need — buying a ticket, asking for directions, and confirming which platform to wait on. Working in pairs, each group was given a small task: find the fastest route to a specific station, or ask an MRT staff member a question and report back the answer.

Several students were nervous about speaking English to strangers at first, but most found that MRT staff were patient and happy to help. By the return journey, students were comparing notes about which English phrases worked best and laughing about a few misunderstandings along the way.

Back in the classroom the following week, students wrote up their travel journals in English, describing what they saw, what they asked, and what surprised them most about navigating a real transport system independently.

title: New English journal issue — Semester 2, 2026
subtitle: Eight student articles published in this issue's English column
excerpt: The latest school journal features eight student articles in English — science reports, poems, and reflections from both Grade 5 and 6 classes.
paragraphs: The latest issue of 青埔兒童 (Qingpu Children), our school magazine, features eight student articles in English — science reports, poems, and reflections from both Grade 5 and 6 classes. This semester's column was edited by a small student committee for the first time, with teachers stepping back into an advisory role.

Highlights include a Grade 6 environmental science feature on a small irrigation channel running beneath the THSR tracks, a collection of seasonal poems written after garden observation sessions, and a lab report investigating how light colour affects plant growth.

The English column has grown steadily since it launched, and this issue is the longest yet. Teachers say the goal isn't perfect grammar — it's giving students a real audience and a reason to care about how clearly they communicate in English.
Printed copies are available from the school office, and selected articles are featured on the Bilingual Education page of this website.

title: Earth Day science fair — Grade 5 presents research
subtitle: Twelve student teams, twelve experiments, one shared theme
excerpt: Twelve Grade 5 student teams set up experiment stations in the gym, presenting their findings on plant growth, water quality, and solar energy in bilingual poster sessions.
paragraphs: Twelve Grade 5 student teams set up experiment stations in the gym, presenting their findings on plant growth, water quality, and solar energy in bilingual poster sessions held to mark Earth Day.

Each team chose a question related to sustainability and designed a simple experiment to investigate it over several weeks — testing how different amounts of water affected seedling growth, comparing energy output from solar panels at different times of day, or measuring water clarity in samples from around the school grounds.

Visiting classes rotated through the gym in small groups, with presenting students explaining their experiment and results in both English and Mandarin. Younger students from Grade 1 and 2 were especially curious about the solar panel demonstrations.
The fair has become an annual fixture in the Grade 5 calendar, building directly toward the bilingual PE, Art, and Integrative Activities curriculum students continue into Grade 6.

title: Semester 2 begins — welcome back
subtitle: New units, an updated calendar, and a message from the principal
excerpt: Semester 2 of the 2025–2026 school year opens today. New bilingual units, an updated school calendar, and a welcome message from the principal.
paragraphs: Semester 2 of the 2025–2026 school year opens today. New bilingual units, an updated school calendar, and a welcome message from the principal greet students and families as classes resume.

This semester brings new integrated activity units across several grades, alongside the continuation of the bilingual PE and Art curriculum for Grade 5 and 6. The updated school calendar — visible on the homepage of this website — includes several new events, including an expanded Earth Day science fair and a second instalment of the English journal column.

In her welcome message, the principal thanked families for their continued support of the bilingual programme and encouraged students to keep practising English outside the classroom, noting that the school's location near THSR Taoyuan Station gives students daily opportunities to use English in real situations.
Office hours and contact details for the new semester are available on the Visit and Contact pages of this website.

title: Qing Pu recognised as Taoyuan Bilingual Model School
subtitle: One of twelve schools selected across the district
excerpt: The Taoyuan City Education Bureau named us one of twelve model bilingual schools in the district for 2025–2026, recognising our curriculum design and student outcomes.
paragraphs: The Taoyuan City Education Bureau named Qing Pu Elementary School one of twelve model bilingual schools in the district for 2025–2026, recognising our curriculum design and student outcomes.

The recognition followed a year-long review process that included classroom observations, teacher interviews, and assessment of student English proficiency across grade levels. Reviewers highlighted the school's EMI-based approach — using English as the medium for teaching subjects like PE, Art, and integrated activities, rather than treating it as an isolated subject.

The bureau's report specifically praised the school's bilingual specialist teaching team, the structured progression of bilingual subjects from Life Studies in lower grades through to PE, Art, and Integrative Activities in upper grades, and the student-led English journal column as evidence of genuine language use beyond the classroom.
As a model school, Qing Pu will host visiting teachers from other Taoyuan schools later this year, sharing curriculum materials and classroom strategies developed over the past several years.

title: Grade 4 visits Taoyuan Museum of Art
subtitle: A guided tour conducted entirely in English
excerpt: A five-minute walk from school led Grade 4 students to the Taoyuan Museum of Art for a guided tour conducted in English by museum educators.
paragraphs: A five-minute walk from school led Grade 4 students to the Taoyuan Museum of Art for a guided tour conducted in English by museum educators, as part of the 'All Together' integrated activities curriculum.

The museum, opened in 2021, sits within easy walking distance of the school and has become a regular destination for classes studying art, architecture, and design. This visit focused on the museum's current exhibition of contemporary Taiwanese sculpture, with the guide encouraging students to describe what they saw using English colour, shape, and texture vocabulary practised in class beforehand.
Students worked in pairs to sketch their favourite piece and write a short English description, which several teachers plan to display in the school's art corridor later this term.

Museum staff noted that Qing Pu students were unusually comfortable asking follow-up questions in English — a small but visible sign, teachers said, of how consistently the bilingual programme builds confidence over time.

title: Bilingual Science Week — Semester 1
subtitle: "Our Planet, Our Responsibility" — a week of English-medium science
excerpt: A week of English-medium science lessons, experiments, and a guest speaker from National Central University explored the theme: "Our Planet, Our Responsibility."
paragraphs: A week of English-medium science lessons, experiments, and a guest speaker from National Central University explored the theme: "Our Planet, Our Responsibility."

Each day of the week focused on a different environmental topic — water conservation, renewable energy, biodiversity, and waste reduction — with Grade 5 and 6 classes conducting hands-on experiments related to each theme entirely in English. A highlight was a Wednesday assembly talk from a National Central University environmental science researcher, who answered student questions about climate research in Taiwan.

Teachers structured the week to build directly on vocabulary and concepts from the regular bilingual Integrative Activities curriculum, giving students an extended opportunity to use academic English outside the constraints of a single lesson period.
Feedback from both students and the visiting researcher was strongly positive, and the school is planning to make Bilingual Science Week an annual fixture each October.

title: New school year opens — welcome to 2025–2026
subtitle: Eighteen classes, an expanded bilingual programme, and new teachers
excerpt: The 2025–2026 school year begins with 18 classes, an expanded bilingual programme in Grade 5–6, and three new certified bilingual subject teachers joining the team.
paragraphs: The 2025–2026 school year begins with 18 classes, an expanded bilingual programme in Grade 5–6, and three new bilingual subject teachers joining the team.

This year's expansion brings PE, Art, and Integrative Activities bilingual lessons to all Grade 5 and 6 classes, building on a successful pilot the previous year. The three new bilingual teachers join the school's existing team of 18 local and 5 foreign teachers, bringing additional subject expertise in PE, Art, and integrated activities.
Families can find full details of the bilingual curriculum by grade, along with introductions to the teaching team, on the Bilingual Education and Teachers pages of this website.
The school calendar for the new year, including key dates for assessments, events, and holidays, is available on the homepage.
